title: ICE Rescinds Biden-Era Rule Requiring Reporting of Detainee Deaths Within 30 Days of Release
excerpt: Acting ICE Director David Venturella issued an internal memo eliminating the agency's requirement to report and investigate deaths of detainees occurring within 30 days of their release — a 2021 safeguard designed to prevent ICE from avoiding accountability by releasing gravely ill detainees before they died. The policy change came as 2026 was on pace to become the deadliest year for ICE detainees in two decades.
content:
On June 4, 2026, Acting ICE Director David Venturella issued an internal memo eliminating a 2021 Biden-era policy that required ICE to report to Congress and investigate detainee deaths occurring within 30 days of release from custody.

  • The 2021 rule was specifically designed to prevent ICE from shedding accountability by releasing critically ill detainees just before death — a practice that had been documented in prior years.
  • Former DHS officials warned the change eliminates a key safeguard: "The period immediately following release is when deaths attributable to inadequate care during confinement become apparent," said one UCSF epidemiologist who studies ICE deaths.
  • At the time of the policy change, ICE had reported 18 detainee deaths in the first five months of 2026 alone — a pace on track to surpass the 32 deaths in 2025, which was the highest annual total since 2004.
  • The detained population had surged to 60,000–70,000 individuals, up from roughly 39,000 at the end of 2024.
  • Health experts warned the change would make it harder to identify failures in detention medical care and would cause mortality statistics to "appear lower without any actual improvement in care."

DHS defended the shift as "common sense," saying that once a person is no longer in ICE custody, the agency is no longer responsible for monitoring or reviewing deaths. Critics noted that the timing — amid record detention deaths and a dismantled oversight infrastructure — raised serious accountability concerns.
mediaPlaceholder: Internal ICE memo from Acting Director Venturella rescinding the 30-day post-release death reporting requirement
imageAlt: ICE Rescinds Biden-Era Rule Requiring Reporting of Detainee Deaths Within 30 Days of Release
